name: Sangrera River Valley Trail
name_original: Valle del río Sangrera (PR-TO 35) — circular desde San Bartolomé de las Abiertas
description: Hike the moderate 10.4-mile Valle del río Sangrera (PR-TO 35) circular trail from San Bartolomé de las Abiertas, with river views.
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around Talavera traverse a landscape characterized by the Tagus River and its tributaries, offering natural areas ideal for discovering cascades. The region, part of Castilla-La Mancha, features a mix of riverine environments and gentle hills, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. These natural settings create opportunities for exploring water features and enjoying the local flora and fauna. Best waterfall hiking trails around Talavera The most popular waterfall hiking route is Ruta de los Pescadores (PRC-ASP-1) — circular desde Arenas de San Pedro, a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows river paths, offering views of the water and surrounding nature. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda de los Pescadores Trail, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) path. This trail winds along the river, providing a tranquil experience through natural woodlands and by water features. Local hikers also love the Fishermen's Trail, a 8.1 miles (13.0 km) trail leading through river valleys and natural areas, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Hiking around Talavera is defined by river paths, natural woodlands, and gentle hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate walks along watercourses to more extended routes. The routes in Talavera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Talavera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails are there around Talavera?
A: There are currently 4 waterfall hiking trails available around Talavera. These routes are generally moderate in difficulty, offering varied terrain for exploration.

Q: Are there any circular waterfall walks in the Talavera area?
A: Yes, there are circular options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318188689/ruta-de-los-pescadores-prc-asp-1-circular-desde-arenas-de-san-pedro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Pescadores (PRC-ASP-1) — circular desde Arenas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route that allows you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of waterfall hikes near Talavera?
A: The waterfall hikes around Talavera vary in length, with routes typically ranging from about 7.3 miles (11.7 km) to 9.3 miles (15.0 km). Most of these trails can be completed in approximately 3 hours 50 minutes to 4 hours 15 minutes, depending on your pace and stops.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the waterfall trails in Talavera?
A: The waterfall trails in Talavera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil river paths, natural woodlands, and the opportunity to discover water features in the region.

Q: Are the waterfall hiking trails around Talavera suitable for families?
A: The trails around Talavera are generally moderate, featuring river paths and gentle hills. While there are no specifically 'easy' routes listed, families with some hiking experience should find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1289777113/senda-de-los-pescadores-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de los Pescadores Trail&lt;/a&gt; enjoyable, offering a tranquil experience through natural woodlands.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the waterfall trails in Talavera?
A: Many natural trails in Spain, especially those in less protected areas, are generally dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control. It's always best to check local signage or specific route details for any restrictions, but the riverine environments of Talavera often welcome well-behaved dogs.

Q: What kind of wildlife and geology can I expect to see around Talavera's waterfalls?
A: The region's riverine environments and natural woodlands, characterized by the Tagus River and its tributaries, support diverse flora and fauna. You might encounter various bird species, small mammals, and a rich variety of plant life typical of central Spain's riparian ecosystems. The geology primarily consists of gentle hills and river valleys.

Q: What should I wear and bring for a waterfall hike in Talavera?
A: For waterfall hikes in Talavera, it's advisable to wear comfortable hiking shoes with good grip, as paths can be uneven or damp near water features. Bring enough water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for the weather. A small backpack for essentials is also recommended.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the waterfall trails?
A: Yes, the Talavera region offers several interesting sights. You can explore historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2745111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Count’s Palace of Oropesa and Oropesa Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5451674" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpica Castle&lt;/a&gt;. For natural beauty, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6116045" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portiña Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4158386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cazalegas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which are also nearby.

Q: Is wild swimming permitted at the waterfalls in Talavera?
A: While the region features many water features and rivers, specific regulations regarding wild swimming at waterfalls can vary. It's important to exercise caution, respect local rules, and be aware of water conditions. Always look for official signage or local guidance before attempting to swim in natural bodies of water.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit Talavera for waterfall hikes?
A: The best time to visit Talavera for waterfall hikes is typically during spring (March to May) or autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant for hiking, and the water levels in the rivers and waterfalls are often at their most impressive after seasonal rains.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs near the waterfall trails for refreshments?
A: While the trails themselves are often in natural, secluded areas, many villages and towns surrounding Talavera offer local cafes and pubs where you can find refreshments before or after your hike. It's a good idea to plan your route and check for amenities in nearby settlements like Arenas de San Pedro or Tinarejo.
tips: Among the monuments of the town, the parish church of San Bartolomé and the town hall stand out. We can also see a powder magazine from the Civil War, which is a military construction, which was also used as an air raid shelter.
This section of the PR-TO 35 trail passes along the banks of the Sangrera River. We can enjoy the richness of the vegetation and the company of waterfowl.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Valle del río Sangrera (PR-TO 35) circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is approximately 16.6 kilometers long and typically takes around 4.5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: The PR-TO 35 trail is classified as having a moderate difficulty. It involves an accumulated elevation gain of 286 meters, making it suitable for individuals who are accustomed to walking and regular hiking.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The circular trail starts and ends in the town of San Bartolomé de las Abiertas. You can usually find parking within the town, close to the trailhead.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery. It begins by traversing olive groves and cereal fields. As you ascend, you'll get panoramic views of the Sangrera River Valley, the Montes de Toledo, and the Sierra de Gredos. The second section follows the river valley with lush riparian vegetation, including ash trees, poplars, and brambles.

faqs.question: What notable features or landmarks can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty, you can observe rural architecture such as farmhouses, threshing floors, wells, and ancient ponds. A point of historical interest is a Civil War gunpowder magazine, believed to have been constructed by the republican rearguard.

faqs.question: Is the Valle del río Sangrera (PR-TO 35) trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife, and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is accessible throughout the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, while winter can be beautiful but cooler. Summer hikes are possible, but it's advisable to start early to avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike the Valle del río Sangrera (PR-TO 35) trail.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities in San Bartolomé de las Abiertas?
faqs.answer: As the starting and ending point, San Bartolomé de las Abiertas is a settlement where you can find basic amenities such as cafes or restaurants to refuel after your hike.

faqs.question: What kind of flora and fauna might I encounter?
faqs.answer: The diverse landscape, from olive groves to riparian forests, supports varied flora and fauna. You'll see typical Mediterranean vegetation and might spot local bird species, small mammals, and insects characteristic of the Tagus valley region.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to San Bartolomé de las Abiertas?
faqs.answer: While direct public transport options might be limited, you can often find bus services to San Bartolomé de las Abiertas from larger nearby towns like Talavera de la Reina. It's best to check local bus schedules in advance.
